zh.lifeSummary: 當Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy於1323年出生在義大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾特左 素利也阿達時，他的父親Stefano Visconti of Milan35歲，母親Lady Valentina Doria of Milan33歲。他於1350年9月27日與Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan在義大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾米蘭諾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和17個女兒的父母。他去世於1385年12月19日在義大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾特左 素利也阿達享年62歲，埋葬在義大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾特左 素利也阿達。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy于1323年出生在意大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾特左 素利也阿達时，他的父亲Stefano Visconti of Milan35岁，母亲Lady Valentina Doria of Milan33岁。他于1350年9月27日与Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan在意大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾米蘭諾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和17个女儿的父母。他去世于1385年12月19日在意大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾特左 素利也阿達享年62岁，埋葬在意大利隆巴迪亞米蘭諾特左 素利也阿達。

nameMeanings.name: Barnabo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament. See Barnabas .

variants.name: Barnabas
meanings: From the New Testament, where Barnabas represents a Greek form of an Aramaic name meaning ‘son of consolation’. St Barnabas was a Jewish Cypriot, one of the earliest Christian missionaries, who sold his property and gave the proceeds to the Church. He worked with St Paul until about ad 48 .

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Diana   and   Diane .

variants.name: Diana
meanings: Name borne in Roman mythology by the goddess of the moon and of hunting, equivalent to the Greek Artemis. In mythology she is characterized as both beautiful and chaste. Her name is of ancient and uncertain derivation. It probably contains a first element that is also found in the name of the Greek god Dionysos ( see Dennis ) and the Latin name of the supreme god Jupiter. It was adopted in Britain during the Tudor period as a learned name, a borrowing from Latin influenced by the French form Diane . Although it was much used by Elizabethan poets celebrating the virgin goddess and alluding to the Virgin Queen, it was not particularly popular as a given name until the end of the 19th century. In earlier centuries some clergymen were reluctant to baptize girls with this pagan name, mindful of the riots against St Paul stirred up by worshippers of Diana of the Ephesians (Acts 19:24–41). In the late 20th century, its popularity received a boost because of its association with the late Diana , Princess of Wales ( 1961–97 ), who was renowned for her beauty, glamour, and compassion.

variants.name: Diane
meanings: form of   Diana  , now also widely used in the English-speaking world. It was especially popular among the Renaissance aristocracy, who loved hunting and were therefore proud to name their daughters after the classical goddess of the chase.

nameMeanings.name: Visconti
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Pasquale, Rocco, Umberto, Amerigo, Angelo, Carlo, Domenic, Ennio, Gaetano, Gerlando, Gino.  French Laurent, Camille, Girard.

Italian: from visconte, a title of rank (medieval Latin vicecomes ‘deputy of a count’). Unusually (since most noble families took their surnames from their estates), the surname was sometimes of literal application, but without doubt it is also in part a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces, and in part a metonymic occupational name for someone in the employ of a viscount. This surname is also found in southern France.

variants.name: Viscomi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Emilio, Salvatore, Antonio, Beniamino, Filomena, Mario, Rodolfo, Santo.
Italian: Sicilian and Calabrian form of Visconti , from medieval Latin viscomes ‘deputy’, Greek biskōmēs.

nameMeanings.name: Signore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Pasquale, Dino, Enzo, Gennaro, Luigi, Marco, Vincenzo.
Italian (southern):
from the medieval personal name Signore (from Latin senior ‘senior, elder’, genitive senioris).

variants.name: Signorino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Gasper, Aldo, Antonio, Gaetano, Salvatore, Santo, Vincenzo.
Italian (Naples): from a diminutive of   Signore  .

variants.name: Signor
meanings: Italian: variant of   Signore  .

variants.name: Signorello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Aldo, Benito, Carmelo, Dino, Gaetano, Gino, Marcello, Marco, Pietro, Rinaldo, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: from a diminutive of   Signore  .

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Diana   and   Diane .

variants.name: Diana
meanings: Name borne in Roman mythology by the goddess of the moon and of hunting, equivalent to the Greek Artemis. In mythology she is characterized as both beautiful and chaste. Her name is of ancient and uncertain derivation. It probably contains a first element that is also found in the name of the Greek god Dionysos ( see Dennis ) and the Latin name of the supreme god Jupiter. It was adopted in Britain during the Tudor period as a learned name, a borrowing from Latin influenced by the French form Diane . Although it was much used by Elizabethan poets celebrating the virgin goddess and alluding to the Virgin Queen, it was not particularly popular as a given name until the end of the 19th century. In earlier centuries some clergymen were reluctant to baptize girls with this pagan name, mindful of the riots against St Paul stirred up by worshippers of Diana of the Ephesians (Acts 19:24–41). In the late 20th century, its popularity received a boost because of its association with the late Diana , Princess of Wales ( 1961–97 ), who was renowned for her beauty, glamour, and compassion.

variants.name: Diane
meanings: form of   Diana  , now also widely used in the English-speaking world. It was especially popular among the Renaissance aristocracy, who loved hunting and were therefore proud to name their daughters after the classical goddess of the chase.

nameMeanings.name: Milano
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Czech: from a short form of various compound names formed with mil ‘grace, favour’, now also used in the English-speaking world. Sometimes, however, it may be chosen with reference to the northern Italian city (Italian Milano), the capital of Lombardy.

nameMeanings.name: Duke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: In modern use this normally represents a coinage parallel to   Earl   and   King  , but it has been used as a short form of   Marmaduke   from at least the early 1600s.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Lombardy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Damien.
Americanized form of Italian   Lombardi  .
French (mainly Puy-de-Dôme): variant of   Lombard  .

variants.name: Lombardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Domenic, Guido, Aldo, Sal, Pasquale, Luigi, Dino.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Lombardo  .

en.lifeSummary: When Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy was born in 1323, in Trezzo sull'Adda, Milan, Lombardy, Italy, his father, Stefano Visconti of Milan, was 35 and his mother, Lady Valentina Doria of Milan, was 33. He married Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan on 27 September 1350, in Milano, Milan, Lombardy, Italy. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 17 daughters. He died on 19 December 1385, in Trezzo sull'Adda, Milan, Lombardy, Italy, at the age of 62, and was buried in Trezzo sull'Adda, Milan, Lombardy, Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy est né en 1323, à Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italie, son père, Stefano Visconti of Milan, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Lady Valentina Doria of Milan, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan le 27 septembre 1350, à Milano, Milano, Lombardia, Italie. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 17 filles. Il est décédé le 19 décembre 1385, à Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italie, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy 1323 in Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardei, Italien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Stefano Visconti of Milan 35 und seine Mutter Lady Valentina Doria of Milan 33. Er hat am 27. September 1350 in Milano, Milano, Lombardei, Italien Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 17 Töchter. Er starb am 19. Dezember 1385 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardei, Italien und wurde in Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardei, Italien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy è nato il nel 1323 nel Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italia, il suo padre, Stefano Visconti of Milan, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Lady Valentina Doria of Milan, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan il 27 settembre 1350, nel Milano, Milano, Lombardia, Italia. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 17 figlie. Morì il 19 dicembre 1385, nel Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italia, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italia.

ja.lifeSummary: Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardyが1323年にイタリアロンバーディアミラノトレズゾ スル'アッダで生まれた時，父Stefano Visconti of Milanは35歳，母Lady Valentina Doria of Milanは33歳であった。1350年9月27日にイタリアロンバーディアミラノミラノでBeatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と17人の娘の親であった。彼は1385年12月19日にイタリアロンバーディアミラノトレズゾ スル'アッダで62歳で死去し，イタリアロンバーディアミラノトレズゾ スル'アッダにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy(이)가 1323년 이탈리아 롬바디아 미랜오 트레즈오 설'아다에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Stefano Visconti of Milan(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Valentina Doria of Milan(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1350년 9월 27일 이탈리아 롬바디아 미랜오 미랜오에서 Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 열 명 이상의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1385년 12월 19일 이탈리아 롬바디아 미랜오 트레즈오 설'아다에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 이탈리아 롬바디아 미랜오 트레즈오 설'아다에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy nasceu em 1323, em Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Itália, seu pai, Stefano Visconti of Milan, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Lady Valentina Doria of Milan, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan em 27 de setembro de 1350, em Milano, Milano, Lombardia, Itália. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 17 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de dezembro de 1385, em Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Itália, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Itália.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy родился в 1323 г. в местности треззо сулл'адда, милано, ломбардя, Италия, его отцу по имени Stefano Visconti of Milan было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Valentina Doria of Milan, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan 27 сентября 1350 г. в местности милано, милано, ломбардя, Италия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 17 дочерей. Он умер 19 декабря 1385 г. в местности треззо сулл'адда, милано, ломбардя, Италия в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности треззо сулл'адда, милано, ломбардя, Италия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Barnabo di Visconti Signore di Milano Duke of Lombardy Duke of Lombardy nació en 1323, en Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italia, su papá, Stefano Visconti of Milan, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Lady Valentina Doria of Milan, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Beatrice Regina Della Scala Lady of Milan el 27 de septiembre de 1350, en Milano, Milano, Lombardia, Italia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 17 hijas. Murió el 19 de diciembre de 1385, en Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italia, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Trezzo sull'Adda, Milano, Lombardia, Italia.
